name: Glassmorphism
description: Frosted glass effect with blur and transparency
implementation: background: rgba(255 255 255 / 0.1); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); border: 1px solid rgba(255 255 255 / 0.2); border-radius: 16px
use_cases: SaaS dashboards fintech cards modern portfolios
do_examples: Cards with subtle blur and light borders floating over gradient backgrounds
dont_examples: Heavy blur on text areas making content unreadable or blur on every element

name: Neumorphism
description: Soft extruded plastic look with subtle shadows
use_cases: Calculators toggles music players minimal UIs
do_examples: Subtle soft shadows on interactive elements like buttons and toggles
dont_examples: Low contrast making elements invisible to users or using on text-heavy content

name: Brutalism
implementation: font-family: monospace; border: 3px solid black; background: #ff0; text-transform: uppercase
use_cases: Creative agencies art portfolios experimental brands
do_examples: Bold typography stark color contrasts raw HTML aesthetic
dont_examples: E-commerce or healthcare where trust and usability are critical

name: Neubrutalism
description: Softened brutalism with thick borders and shadows
implementation: border: 2px solid #1a1a1a; box-shadow: 4px 4px 0 #1a1a1a; border-radius: 8px; background: pastel
use_cases: Startup landing pages creative tools indie products
do_examples: Thick borders with offset shadows on cards and buttons with pastel backgrounds
dont_examples: Corporate B2B or medical apps where playfulness is inappropriate

name: Minimalism
do_examples: Generous whitespace single-column layouts focused typography
dont_examples: Content-heavy dashboards or e-commerce where density matters

name: Dark Mode
description: Dark backgrounds with light text and colored accents
use_cases: Developer tools fintech gaming analytics dashboards
do_examples: Dark backgrounds with proper contrast ratios and vibrant accent colors
dont_examples: Pure black backgrounds (#000) causing OLED smearing or insufficient contrast

name: Bento Grid
description: Asymmetric grid layout inspired by bento boxes
implementation: display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4 1fr); gap: 16px; with varying spans
do_examples: Different sized cards highlighting key features with visual hierarchy
dont_examples: All cards same size defeating the purpose or too many small cells

name: Gradient Mesh
implementation: background: linear-gradient(135deg #667eea 0% #764ba2 100%); or mesh gradient tools
use_cases: AI products creative tools modern SaaS
do_examples: Subtle gradient backgrounds behind content sections or on hero areas
dont_examples: Gradients on text making it unreadable or too many competing gradients

name: Flat Design
description: No shadows or depth pure 2D aesthetic
implementation: No box-shadow no gradients solid colors sharp edges
use_cases: Mobile apps simple tools content platforms
do_examples: Clean solid color buttons with clear hover states and consistent iconography
dont_examples: Adding shadows or depth elements that break the flat aesthetic

name: Material Design
description: Google design system with elevation and motion
do_examples: Consistent elevation with ripple feedback on interactive elements
dont_examples: Mixing material with other design systems or ignoring elevation rules

name: Skeuomorphism
implementation: Realistic shadows textures gradients to mimic physical objects
use_cases: Specific niche apps music production retro
do_examples: Subtle realistic touches on specific interactive elements
dont_examples: Full skeuomorphic design on modern web apps looks dated

name: Claymorphism
implementation: border-radius: 24px; background: pastel; box-shadow: inset 2px 2px 4px light outer shadow
do_examples: Soft rounded elements with inner shadows creating 3D clay effect
dont_examples: Professional B2B or enterprise where playfulness is inappropriate

name: Retro/Vintage
implementation: Muted color palettes serif fonts grain textures vintage illustrations
use_cases: Craft brands artisan products nostalgic brands
do_examples: Consistent era-specific color palette typography and imagery style
dont_examples: Mixing retro with modern elements creating confused visual identity

name: Corporate Clean
implementation: System fonts or safe web fonts blue/gray palette generous whitespace
do_examples: Professional color palette clear hierarchy accessibility-first
dont_examples: Trying to be trendy with corporate audiences who value stability

name: Organic/Natural
description: Nature-inspired with soft shapes and earth tones
implementation: border-radius: 50% blob shapes; earth tone palette; natural textures
do_examples: Soft organic shapes with nature-inspired imagery and earth tones
dont_examples: Sharp geometric shapes that contradict the organic brand message
